SerialPort.description: Use SerialPort class and declare variables to create a COM port device. The variable name will be the device name.
SerialPort.constructor: SerialPort <variableName> = string, int, string, int, float, int, bool, bool, bool

Socket.description: Use Socket class and declare variables to create a TCP/IP communication device. The variable name will be the device name.

MDesicion.description: Use the MDecision class with variable declaration to allow users to configure M-Decision nodes for conditional judgments or to display pop-up messages.

Reset.documentation: Reset all MDecision parameters to the default.

TimeoutDefaultCase.documentation: The default index after the timeout

Case.documentation: Add, delete, or modify the conditional judgement
parameters: int - Condition or the index of the button

TPoint.description: Use TPoint Class and declare variables to create the point names and the point values. The variable name will be the point name.
constructor: TPoint <variableName> = float, float, float, float, float, float
TPoint <variableName> = string, float[], float[], string, string

TBase.description: Use TBase Class and declare variables to create the point names and the point values. The variable name will be the point name.
constructor: TBase <variableName> = float, float, float, float, float, float

parameters: float - Base Value: X(mm), Y(mm), Z(mm), RX(°), RY(°), RZ(°)

ConShift.documentation: Convert a new base value with the base value (the original base teach value) and the shift value, where the shift depends on the position and direction of the original coordinate.
parameters: float[] - Shift Value: X(mm), Y(mm), Z(mm), RX(°), RY(°), RZ(°)

TTCP.description: Use TTCP Class and declare variables to create the point names and the point values. The variable name will be the point name.

ModbusTCP.description: Modbus TCP device class. Variable name becomes device name.

IODDPreset.documentation: Read IODD file and configure preset parameters.

ModbusRTU.description: Modbus RTU device class. Variable name becomes device name.
constructor: ModbusRTU <variableName> = string, int, string, int, float
ModbusRTU <variableName> = string, int, string, int, float, int
ModbusRTU <variableName> = string, int, string, int, float, int, bool, bool, bool

IODDPreset.documentation: Read IODD file and configure preset parameters.

Compliance.description: Compliance class for robot compliance control settings and stop conditions. Provides single axis, teach, multiple axes, and impedance compliance control modes.

Reset.documentation: Reset all the compliance control motion parameters to the default.

Frame.documentation: Set the reference base of the compliance control motion. 1=Tool, 2=Current base.

HighResistance.documentation: Set the resistance on the non-motion direction (X,Y,Z). false=Default, true=High resistance.

Single.documentation: Set the single axis parameters of compliance control motion.
parameters: int - Distance (mm for XYZ, degree for RXRYRZ)

Teach.documentation: Set the teach parameters of compliance control motion.

Multiple.documentation: Set the multiple axes parameters of compliance control motion.

Impedance.documentation: Set the impedance parameters of compliance control motion.

Timeout.documentation: Set the stop condition of timeout. <0=Disable, >=0=Timeout duration in ms. Call with no params to cancel.

DInput.documentation: Set the stop condition of the digital input. Call with no params to cancel.

AInput.documentation: Set the stop condition of the analog input. Call with no params to cancel.

Condition.documentation: Set the stop condition of the conditional expression. Call with no params to cancel.

Start.documentation: Start the compliance control motion. Returns result: 0=Not Working, 1=Working, 2=Timeout, 3=Distance reached, 4=IO triggered, 5=Resisted, 6=Error, 14=Over Speed, 201-206=Various stop conditions.

Stop.documentation: Stop the compliance control motion. Returns result value after compliance control motion stops.

TouchStop.description: TouchStop class for robot touch stop control settings and stop conditions. Supports Compliance, Line, and Force operation modes.

Reset.documentation: Reset all the touchstop control motion parameters to the default except the operation mode and sensor device.

Frame.documentation: Set the reference base of the touchstop control motion. 1=Tool, 2=Current base.

HighResistance.documentation: Set the resistance on the non-motion direction (X,Y,Z). Valid when operation mode is Compliance.

BrakeDistance.documentation: Set the braking distance in mm. Valid when operation mode is Line.

RecordPosPoint.documentation: Set the record touchstop coordinate value to the point name. Call with no params to cancel.
parameters: bool - Record trigger point (false=stop point, true=trigger point)

Single.documentation: Set the single axis parameters of touchstop control motion.

Teach.documentation: Set the teach parameters of touchstop control motion.

AdvSet.documentation: Set the advanced parameters of touchstop control motion. Valid when operation mode is Compliance.

Timeout.documentation: Set the stop condition of timeout. <0=Disable, >=0=Timeout duration in ms. Call with no params to cancel.

DInput.documentation: Set the stop condition of the digital input. Can also set stroke ratio for Compliance mode. Call with no params to cancel.

AInput.documentation: Set the stop condition of the analog input. Call with no params to cancel.

Condition.documentation: Set the stop condition of the conditional expression. Call with no params to cancel.

Resisted.documentation: Set the stop condition of external resistance detection. Valid when operation mode is Compliance. Call with no params to cancel.

FTReached.documentation: Set the stop condition of monitoring force/torque values. Valid when operation mode is Force. Call with no params to cancel.

Start.documentation: Start the touchstop control motion. Returns result: 0=Not Working, 1=Working, 2=Timeout, 3=Distance reached, 4=IO triggered, 5=Resisted, 6=Error, 14=Over Speed, 201-206=Various stop conditions.
parameters: bool - Zero out force sensor before execution (for Force mode)

Stop.documentation: Stop the touchstop control motion. Returns result value after touchstop control motion stops.

GetStoppedPos.documentation: Get the stopped position values after the robot touch-stopped. Returns float[] of coordinates or joint angles.
parameters: int - Coordinate type (0=flange by current base, 1=joint angle, 2=TCP by current base, 3=TCP by RobotBase, 4=end TCP by RobotBase)

GetTriggeredPos.documentation: Get the triggered position values after the robot touch-stopped. Returns float[] of coordinates or joint angles.
parameters: int - Coordinate type (0=flange by current base, 1=joint angle, 2=TCP by current base, 3=TCP by RobotBase, 4=end TCP by RobotBase)

GetMovingDistance.documentation: Get the moving distance of the touchstop motion in mm.

FTSensor.description: FTSensor class for Force Torque Sensor device. Supports TMFT300, OnRobot_HEX-E/H, ROBOTIQ_FT300, SCHUNK_Axia80, SRI_M4313N3C, WACOH sensors.
constructor: FTSensor <variableName> = string, string, float[], float[], float

X.description: The strength value of the X axis

Y.description: The strength value of the Y axis

Z.description: The strength value of the Z axis

TX.description: The torque value of the X axis

TY.description: The torque value of the Y axis

TZ.description: The torque value of the Z axis

Value.description: The XYZ force strength value and torque value array {X, Y, Z, TX, TY, TZ}, Size = 6

ForceValue.description: The XYZ force strength value array {X, Y, Z}, Size = 3

TorqueValue.description: The XYZ torque value array {TX, TY, TZ}, Size = 3

RefCoordX.description: The X-axis strength value measured based on the reference base set in the node

RefCoordY.description: The Y-axis strength value measured based on the reference base set in the node

RefCoordZ.description: The Z-axis strength value measured based on the reference base set in the node

RefCoordTX.description: The X-axis torque value measured based on the reference base set in the node

RefCoordTY.description: The Y-axis torque value measured based on the reference base set in the node

RefCoordTZ.description: The Z-axis torque value measured based on the reference base set in the node

RefCoordF3D.description: The XYZ strength measured based on the reference base set in the node

RefCoordT3D.description: The XYZ torque measured based on the reference base set in the node

RefCoordForceValue.description: The XYZ strength value matrix measured based on the reference base {RefCoordX, RefCoordY, RefCoordZ}, Size = 3

RefCoordTorqueValue.description: The XYZ torque value matrix measured based on the reference base {RefCoordTX, RefCoordTY, RefCoordTZ}, Size = 3

Model.description: The Model name of the F/T sensor

Zero.description: Turn on or off F/T sensor offset. 0=Zero OFF, 1=Zero ON

Open.documentation: Open the connection to the Force Torque Sensor. Returns true on success, false on failure.

Close.documentation: Close the connection from the Force Torque Sensor. Returns true on success, false on failure.

Force.description: Force class for robot force control settings and motions. Used with FTSensor for force/torque-based control.

Reset.documentation: Reset all the force control motion parameters to the default except the sensor name.

Frame.documentation: Set the reference base of the force control motion. 0=Robot base, 1=Tool (default), 2=Current base, 3=Trajectory.

Distance.documentation: Set the moving distance of the force control motion in mm. Available only in SetPoint F/T operation mode. <0=No limit, >=0=Limited distance.

ProtectionSpeed.documentation: Set the speed protection of the force control motion against axial force or torque.

FTSet.documentation: Set the force and torque of the force control motion. Positive/negative values denote force control direction. Can adjust PID control parameters.

Trajectory.documentation: Set the trajectory path of the force control motion. Trajectory F/T operation mode. Use subflow name or customized function.
parameters: string/? - Subflow name or trajectory path function

Timeout.documentation: Set the stop condition of timeout in milliseconds. Available only in SetPoint F/T operation mode. <0=Disable, >=0=Timeout duration. Call with no params to cancel.

AllowPosTol.documentation: Set the stop condition of tolerant position error in mm. <0=Disable, >=0=Distance of tolerant error. Call with no params to cancel.

DInput.documentation: Set the stop condition of the digital input. Call with no params to cancel.

AInput.documentation: Set the stop condition of the analog input. Call with no params to cancel.

FTReached.documentation: Set the stop condition of monitoring force/torque values. Call with no params to cancel.

Condition.documentation: Set the stop condition of the conditional expression. Call with no params to cancel.

Start.documentation: Start the force control motion. Returns result: 0=Not Working, 1=Working, 2=Timeout, 3=Distance reached, 4=IO triggered, 5=Resisted, 6=Error, 14=Over Speed, 201-206=Various stop conditions.
parameters: bool - Zero out force sensor before execution (default true)
bool - Enable tool gravity compensation (default false)

Stop.documentation: Stop the force control motion. Returns result value after force control motion stops.

Point.description: Access point data in the project. Syntax: Point["name"].attribute

Value.description: The coordinate of the point {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

Joint.description: The joint angle {J1, J2, J3, J4, J5, J6}, Size = 6

Pose.description: The pose of the robot {Config1, Config2, Config3}, Size = 3

Flange.description: The coordinate of the flange's center {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

TeachValue.description: The original coordinate of the teaching point {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

TeachJoint.description: The joint angle at the teach point {J1, J2, J3, J4, J5, J6}, Size = 6

TeachPose.description: The original pose of the robot on the teaching point {Config1, Config2, Config3}, Size = 3

Base.description: Access base coordinate data. Syntax: Base["name"].attribute or Base["name", int].attribute

Value.description: The base value {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

Type.description: The type of the base: 'R'=Robot Base, 'V'=Vision Base, 'C'=Custom Base

TeachValue.description: The original teach base value {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

TCP.description: Access TCP (Tool Center Point) data. Syntax: TCP["name"].attribute

Value.description: The value of the TCP {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

Mass.description: The value of mass in kg

MOI.description: The value of the Principal Moments of Inertia {Ixx, Iyy, Izz}, Size = 3

MCF.description: The value of Mass center frame with principle axes w.r.t tool frame {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

TeachValue.description: The original value of the TCP {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

TeachMass.description: The original value of mass in kg

TeachMOI.description: The original value of the Principal Moments of Inertia {Ixx, Iyy, Izz}, Size = 3

TeachMCF.description: The original value of Mass center frame {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

VPoint.description: Access vision point data. Syntax: VPoint["jobName"].attribute

Value.description: The initial coordinate of VPoint {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

BaseName.description: The name of the VPoint base

TeachValue.description: The original job initial coordinate of VPoint {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

IO.description: Access digital and analog I/O. Syntax: IO["moduleName"].attribute. Modules: ControlBox, EndModule, ExtModule0..N, Safety

Robot.description: Access robot status and configuration. Syntax: Robot[0].attribute (index fixed at 0)

CoordRobot.description: TCP coordinate relative to RobotBase {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

CoordBase.description: TCP coordinate relative to current base {X, Y, Z, RX, RY, RZ}, Size = 6

Joint.description: Current robot joint angles {J1, J2, J3, J4, J5, J6}, Size = 6

BaseName.description: The name of the current base

TCPName.description: The name of the current TCP

Payload.description: The current compensatory payload in kg

CameraLight.description: The lighting of the robot's camera. 0=Off, 1=On

InstantCameraLight.description: The lighting of the robot's camera (Instant Command). 0=Off, 1=On

TCPForce3D.description: Current TCP force as composite of x, y, z in N

TCPSpeed3D.description: Current TCP speed as composite of x, y, z in mm/s

FT.description: Access force/torque sensor data. Syntax: FT["sensorName"].attribute

X.description: The force value of the X axis

Y.description: The force value of the Y axis

Z.description: The force value of the Z axis

TX.description: The torque value of the X axis

TY.description: The torque value of the Y axis

TZ.description: The torque value of the Z axis

Value.description: The XYZ force and torque array {X, Y, Z, TX, TY, TZ}, Size = 6

ForceValue.description: The XYZ force value array {X, Y, Z}, Size = 3

TorqueValue.description: The XYZ torque value array {TX, TY, TZ}, Size = 3

RefCoordX.description: X-axis force based on reference base (Force Control only)

RefCoordY.description: Y-axis force based on reference base (Force Control only)

RefCoordZ.description: Z-axis force based on reference base (Force Control only)

RefCoordTX.description: X-axis torque based on reference base (Force Control only)

RefCoordTY.description: Y-axis torque based on reference base (Force Control only)

RefCoordTZ.description: Z-axis torque based on reference base (Force Control only)

RefCoordF3D.description: XYZ resultant force based on reference base (Force Control only)

RefCoordT3D.description: XYZ resultant torque based on reference base (Force Control only)

RefCoordForceValue.description: XYZ force array based on reference base {X, Y, Z}, Size = 3

RefCoordTorqueValue.description: XYZ torque array based on reference base {TX, TY, TZ}, Size = 3

Model.description: The model name of the F/T sensor

Zero.description: Turn on or off F/T sensor offset. 0=Zero OFF, 1=Zero ON

Env.description: Access current application environment status. Syntax: Env.attribute (no index required)

AppType.description: Application Type: 1=Real Robot, 2=Virtual Robot, 3=TM AI+ AOI Edge

Byte_ToInt16.documentation: Transform the first two bytes of the assigned byte array to integer, and returns in int type.

Byte_ToInt32.documentation: Transform the first four bytes of byte array to integer, and return in int type.

Byte_ToFloat.documentation: Transform the first four bytes of byte array to floating-point number, and return in floating-point type.

Byte_ToDouble.documentation: Transform the first eight bytes of byte array to floating-point number, and return in double type.

Byte_ToInt16Array.documentation: Transform byte array to integer every 2 bytes, and return in int[] type.

Byte_ToInt32Array.documentation: Transform byte array to integer every 4 bytes, and return in int[] type.

Byte_ToFloatArray.documentation: Transform byte array to integer every 4 bytes, and return in float[] type.

Byte_ToDoubleArray.documentation: Transform byte array to double every 8 bytes, and return in double[] type.

signatures: int - Encoding Rule (0=UTF8 (Default) (0x00 END), 1=HEX BINARY, 2=ASCII (0x00 END))

Byte_Concat.documentation: Concatenate two byte arrays, or concatenate one array with a byte value.
signatures: byte[] - The byte value concatenated after the byte array
byte[] - The input byte array2, concatenated to the end of array1
byte[] - The input byte array2, concatenated to the end of array1
int - Number of element in array2 to be concatenated: 0 to len(array2)
int - Starting index of array1: 0 to len(array1)
int - Number of element in array1 to be concatenated: 0 to len(array1)
byte[] - The input byte array2, concatenated to the end of array1
int - Starting index of array2: 0 to len(array2)
int - Number of element in array2 to be concatenated: 0 to len(array2)
byte or byte[] - The input byte array or value
byte or byte[] - The input byte array or value

String_ToDouble.documentation: Transform string to floating-point number (double type)

String_IndexOf.documentation: Search the address of the first occurrence of a specified strin from left to right.
signatures: string - The specified string to search
int - The initial address to start searching (0 to len(string))
string - The specified string to search
string - The specified string to search
int - The initial address to start searching (0 to len(string))
string - The specified string to search

String_LastIndexOf.documentation: Search the address of the last occurrence of a specified strin from right to left.
signatures: string - The specified string to search
int - The initial address to start searching (0 to len(string))
string - The specified string to search
string - The specified string to search
int - The initial address to start searching (0 to len(string))
string - The specified string to search

String_DiffIndexOf.documentation: Compare the address where the first string difference occurs starting from the start address.
signatures: int - Initial address to start comparing (0 to len(string1))

String_Substring.documentation: Retrieve a substring from input string
signatures: int - The starting character of string
int - The starting character of string
int - The target string to be searched, the substring will start at its position, if it is found
int - The target string to be searched, the substring will start at its position, if it is found

String_Split.documentation: Split the string using specified separator.

String_Replace.documentation: Return a new string in which all occurrences of a specified string in the input string are replaced with another specified string
signatures: string - String to replace all occurrences of old value

String_Trim.documentation: Return a new string in which all leading and trailing occurrences of specified characters or white-space characters from the input string are removed
signatures: string - Specified characters to be removed from leading occurrences
string - Characters to be removed from leading occurrences
string - Characters to be removed from trailing occurrences

String_ToLower.documentation: Change all the characters in a string to lower case

String_ToUpper.documentation: Change all the characters in a string to upper case

Array_Append.documentation: Add new data as the elements in the end of the array.
signatures: ?[] - Data or the array to add

Array_Insert.documentation: Insert data as the elements in the array.
signatures: ?[] - Data or the array to add

Array_Remove.documentation: Delete data as the elements in the array.

Array_Equals.documentation: Determine whether the specified two arrays are identical

Array_IndexOf.documentation: Search for the index number of the first occurrence within array elements.
signatures: int - Start index of the array

Array_LastIndexOf.documentation: Search for the index number of the last occurrence within array elements.
signatures: int - Start index of the array

Array_Reverse.documentation: Reverse the sequence of the elements in the array
signatures: int - Elements to be viewed as a section (2,4,8)

Array_Sort.documentation: Sort the elements in a array

Array_SubElements.documentation: Retrieve the sub-elements from input array
signatures: int - Number of element in sub-elements
int - Number of element in sub-elements

ValueReverse.documentation: Reverse the sequence of byte units inside input data (int 2 bytes or 4 bytes, float 4 bytes, double 8 bytes); or reverse the sequence of character of string.
signatures: int - input value follows int32 or int16 format (0=int32 (Default) ,1=int16 can reverte to int32,2=int16 forced)

GetBytes.documentation: Convert arbitrary data type to byte array.

GetString.documentation: Convert arbitrary data type to string

GetToken.documentation: Retrieve a substring from input string, or the sub-array from the input byte[] array
signatures: int - Number of substring to retrieve
int - Number of substring to retrieve
int - Number of substring to retrieve
int - Number of substring to retrieve
int - Number of substring to retrieve
int - Number of substring to retrieve
int - Number of substring to retrieve
int - Number of substring to retrieve

GetAllTokens.documentation: Retrieve all the substrings from input string, which meets the given condition

signatures: string - The date and time format

GetNowStamp.documentation: Get the total run time or difference in total run time
signatures: bool - Use double format to record project's total run time
int - Previous recorded run time in ms
double - Previous recorded run time in ms
int - Previous recorded run time in ms
int - The expected run time difference
double - Previous recorded run time in ms
double - The expected run time difference

GetVarValue.documentation: Retrieve the value of the variable value. Users can use the string to combine the variable names, and then retrieve the combined value of the variables.

SetVarValue.documentation: For variable value settings, users can use a string to combine the variable names to set the variable value of the combined variable names.

Length.documentation: Acquire the number of byte of input data, length of string or length of array (number of elements in array)

Ctrl.documentation: Change the integer or string to control characters

XOR8.documentation: Utilize XOR 8 bits algorithm to compute the checksum

SUM8.documentation: Utilize SUM 8 bits algorithm to compute the checksum

SUM16.documentation: Utilize SUM 16 bits algorithm to compute the checksum

SUM32.documentation: Utilize SUM 32 bits algorithm to compute the checksum

CRC16.documentation: Utilize CRC 16 bits algorithm to compute the checksum
signatures: int - Number of elements to be computed
int - Number of elements to be computed

CRC32.documentation: Utilize CRC 32 bits algorithm to compute the checksum
signatures: int - Number of elements to be computed

ListenPacket.documentation: Pack the string contents as the compatible protocol for the Listen Node (External Script Control Mode)
signatures: string - The data section in Listen Node communication format
string - The data section in Listen Node communication format

ListenSend.documentation: Send TMSTA, the communication protocol of Listen node, to the client devices connected to the Listen Server currently.

VarSync.documentation: Send the Variable object to TMmanager (Robot Management System)
signatures: ? - Name of variables to be sent
? - Name of variables to be sent
? - Name of variables to be sent
bool - Wait for the end of the motion
bool - Wait for the end of the motion

Pause.documentation: Pause the project and the motion of the robot other than non-paused threads and external script processes.

Resume.documentation: Resume the project and the motion of the robot.

WaitFor.documentation: The loop wait condition stands or time out on waiting.

Sleep.documentation: Stop the thread in the time specified. Same as WaitFor(int).

Display.documentation: Display contents on the TMflow dashboard.

ExecText.documentation: Check and execute the string content input, which must comply with the TMscript definition.
signatures: string - The string content to execute

ExecFile.documentation: Check and execute the file path input, where the string content read from the file must comply with the TMscript definition.
signatures: string - The file path to execute

RedirectToProject.documentation: Stop the currently running project and proceed to execute the specified project.

abs.documentation: Return the absolute value of the designate number

pow.documentation: Return the power of the designate base and exponent

sqrt.documentation: Return the square root of the designate number

ceil.documentation: Return a number rounded upward to its nearest integer.

floor.documentation: Return a number rounded downward to its nearest integer.

round.documentation: Return a number rounded to its nearest integer.
signatures: int - digits after the returned decimal point
int - digits after the returned decimal point

random.documentation: Return a random value in float between 0 and 1 or in an integer between the lower bound and the upper bound.
signatures: int - Upper bound of random value
int - Upper bound of random value
int - Lower bound of random value

sum.documentation: Return the sum of the given numbers or the array of numbers.

average.documentation: Return the average of the given numbers or the array of numbers.

min.documentation: Return the minimum of the given numbers or the array of numbers.

max.documentation: Return the maximum of the given numbers or the array of numbers.

d2r.documentation: Convert the value of degree to radian
signatures: float - Input the value of degree in float
double - Input the value of degree in double

r2d.documentation: Convert the value of radian to degree
signatures: float - Input the value of radian in float
double - Input the value of radian in double

sin.documentation: Return the sine of the input value of degree
signatures: float - Input the value of degree in float
double - Input the value of degree in double

cos.documentation: Return the cosine of the input value of degree
signatures: float - Input the value of degree in float
double - Input the value of degree in double

tan.documentation: Return the tangent of the input value of degree
signatures: float - Input the value of degree in float
double - Input the value of degree in double

asin.documentation: Return the arcsine of the input value in degree
signatures: float - Input the sine value in float
double - Input the sine value in double

acos.documentation: Return the arccosine of the input value in degree
signatures: float - Input the cosine value in float
double - Input the cosine value in double

atan.documentation: Return the arctangent of the input value in degree
signatures: float - Input the cosine value in float
double - Input the cosine value in double

atan2.documentation: Return the arctangent of the quotient of it arguments

log.documentation: Return the natural logarithm of the input value

log10.documentation: Return the logarithm of the input value with the base 10

norm2.documentation: Return the second norm of a specified vector.

dist.documentation: Return the distance between the two coordinates.

trans.documentation: Return the displacement and rotation angle from one specified point to another point.

inversetrans.documentation: Return the displacement and rotation angle {x, y, z, rx, ry, rz} opposite to the input displacement and rotation angle {x, y, z, rx, ry, rz}.

applytrans.documentation: Return the terminal point computed by applied the displacement and rotation angle to the specified point.

interpoint.documentation: Return the interpolate point between two points according to the specified points and ratio

changeref.documentation: Return the new coordinate value described with the new base converted from the original coordinate value
signatures: float[] - Coordinate value of the original point
float[] - Coordinate value of the original point

points2coord.documentation: Calculate the coordinate plane of the input points and return the values of the plane parameters converted by the three points on the plane
signatures: float[] - Origin coordinates in the coordinate plane
float[] - Any point on the X axis in the plane
float[] - Any point on the +X and +Y in the plane

intercoord.documentation: Convert two input planes into a new plane.

coorshift.documentation: Calculate a new coordinate by adding a shift value to the original coordinate, where the shift depends on the position and direction of the original coordinate.

inversekin.documentation: Convert the Cartesian coordinates to joint angles.

forwardkin.documentation: Convert joint angles to the Cartesian coordinates.
signatures: float[] - Joint angle elements to be converted

joint2pose.documentation: Convert joint angles to the Cartesian coordinates.
signatures: float[] - Joint angle elements to be converted

File_ReadBytes.documentation: Read the file content and return in the type of byte[].

File_ReadText.documentation: Read the file content and return in the type of string.

File_ReadLines.documentation: Read the file content and return in the type of string separated by new line characters.

File_NextLine.documentation: Record the last read file path, and continue to read the next line of the file content or open the file to read.
signatures: bool - Open the file to read

File_NextEOF.documentation: Try the last read file path for reading to the end of the file already.

File_WriteBytes.documentation: Put the data content into an array in byte and write to a file.

File_WriteText.documentation: Put the data content in a string and write to a file.

File_WriteLine.documentation: Put the data content in a string with newline characters (0x0D 0x0A) in the end and write to a file.

File_WriteLines.documentation: Put the data content in a string array with newline characters (0x0D 0x0A) in the end and write to a file.

File_Exists.documentation: Check the file path for availability.

File_Replace.documentation: Replace and overwrite the string in the file with a specified string.

File_GetToken.documentation: Read the file by the string pattern and retrieve the substring in the string.

File_GetAllTokens.documentation: Read the file by the string pattern and retrieve all eligible substrings.

File_GetFiles.documentation: Retrieve file names in the assigned directory.

File_LogWrite.documentation: Write the log file that messages are available on the view page of the flowchart.

com_read.documentation: Read data in the Serial Port received buffer and return an array byte[].

com_read_string.documentation: Read the data in the Serial Port buffer, and convert the data to a UTF8 string.
signatures: int - Number of characters to read
int - Number of characters to read

com_write.documentation: Write data to the Serial Port. Numeric values are converted in Little Endian, string values in UTF8.

com_writeline.documentation: Write data to the Serial Port and add line break symbols (0x0D 0x0A) at the end automatically.

socket_read.documentation: Read data in the Received Buffer and return in a byte array.
signatures: int - Number of bytes to read
int - Number of bytes to read

socket_read_string.documentation: Read data in the Received Buffer and convert the byte array to string text in UTF8.
signatures: int - Number of characters to read
int - Number of characters to read

socket_send.documentation: Send data to a remote device. Returns: 1=success, 0=empty data, -1=socket exception, -2=connection failed, -3=device not found.

socket_sendline.documentation: Send data and add line break symbols (0x0D 0x0A) to a remote device. Returns: 1=success, 0=empty data, -1=socket exception, -2=connection failed, -3=device not found.

QueueTag.documentation: Set robot motions with Queue Tag Numbers to denote the current robot motion in process. The status of each queue tag can be monitored using TMSTA SubCmd 01.
signatures: int - Wait for the tagging to continue processing or not

WaitQueueTag.documentation: Wait for the Queue Tag Number of the robot motion to complete.

CheckQueueTag.documentation: Check the Queue Tag Number of the robot motion to complete.

StopAndClearBuffer.documentation: Stop the motion of the robot and clear existing commands of the robot in the buffer.

ChangeBase.documentation: Set the reference base for the consecutive motion.

ChangeTCP.documentation: Set the end tool parameters for the consecutive motion.
signatures: float[] - TCP rotation inertia includes nine values

ChangeLoad.documentation: Set the load weight to compensate.
signatures: float - Weight Conversion Estimated Distance in millimeters
float - Weight Conversion Estimated Distance in millimeters

PTP.documentation: Define and send PTP motion command into buffer for execution. Data format combines three letters: #1 Motion target (C=Cartesian, J=Joint), #2 Speed format (P=percentage), #3 Blending format (P=percentage).

Move_PTP.documentation: Define and send PTP relative motion commands for execution. Data format: #1 Relative target (C=current base, T=tool, J=joint), #2 Speed (P=percentage), #3 Blending (P=percentage).

Line.documentation: Define and send Line motion command into buffer for execution. Data format: #1 Target (C=Cartesian), #2 Speed (P=percentage, A=absolute sync, D=absolute no sync), #3 Blending (P=percentage, R=radius).

Move_Line.documentation: Define and send Line relative motion commands for execution. Data format: #1 Relative target (C=current base, T=tool), #2 Speed (P/A/D), #3 Blending (P/R).

Circle.documentation: Define and send Circle motion command into buffer for execution. Data format: #1 Target (C=Cartesian), #2 Speed (P/A/D), #3 Blending (P=percentage).

PLine.documentation: Define and send PLine motion command into buffer for execution. Data format: #1 Target (J=joint, C=Cartesian), #2 Speed (A/D), #3 Blending (P=percentage).

Move_PLine.documentation: Define and send PLine relative motion commands for execution. Data format: #1 Relative target, #2 Speed (A/D), #3 Blending (P=percentage).

LineSingularity.documentation: Set the line motion control with singularity handling or not. When the project is running, it defaults to 0 to disable.
signatures: int - Singularity handling (0=disable, 1=path avoidance, 2=speed change for TM25S/TM30S)

CollisionCheck.documentation: Pre-collision checks help prevent robots from interfering with self-testing and avoid collisions. Returns empty string if no collision, or the collision location if detected.

PVTEnter.documentation: Set PVT mode to start with Joint/Cartesian command. Before switching to PVT mode, it waits for other robot motion instructions to complete.

PVTExit.documentation: Set PVT mode motion to exit. After exiting PVT mode, it waits for PVT function motion instructions to complete.

PVTPoint.documentation: Set the PVT mode parameters of motion in position, velocity, and duration.

PVTPause.documentation: Set PVT mode motion to pause.

PVTResume.documentation: Set PVT mode motion to resume.

PathOffset_Set.documentation: Set Path Offset parameters. Offset reference base: 0=robot base, 1=tool (default), 2=current base, 3=trajectory.

PathOffset_Get.documentation: Get the Path Offset values. Returns offset values {X,Y,Z,RX,RY,RZ}.
signatures: int - Get mode (0=by robot base, 1=by offset reference base)
void - No parameter (defaults to offset reference base)

PathOffset_IsEnabled.documentation: Check if the Path Offset is enabled.

PathOffset_AlphaFilter.documentation: Set the Alpha Filter coefficient to avoid over-acceleration. Value ranges from 0 to 1. Lower values mean slower offset compensation speed.

PathOffset_MaxOffset.documentation: Set the upper bounds of the distance and the rotation for Path Offset.

Velocity.documentation: Define and activate continuous speed control mode. Control modes: C=linear speed w.r.t. current base, T=linear speed w.r.t. tool base, J=angular speed with joint angles.
signatures: int - Time to top speed (150-2000 ms)

Position.documentation: Define and activate continuous position control mode. Control modes: C=target coordinate w.r.t. current base, T=target coordinate w.r.t. tool base, J=target angle with joint angle.
signatures: int - Time to top speed (150-2000 ms)
int - Expected control value protection time (ms)

SetTCPSpeedLimit.documentation: Set Linear Speed Limit and Rotation Speed Limit for Line Motion.

SetAccTable.documentation: Set the acceleration table. The system will automatically fetch speed and time to top speed from the table by distance. Available for TM Robot S series models.

GetAccTable.documentation: Retrieve the contents of the acceleration table. Returns array of 6 values: Distance Min, Distance Max, Speed 1, Speed 2, Time to Top Speed 1, Time to Top Speed 2. Available for TM Robot S series models.

Vision_DoJob.documentation: Execute the existing vision jobs in the project but not the ones with vision capture points. Returns true if vision job completed and result is pass.

Vision_DoJob_PTP.documentation: Execute the existing vision jobs in the project, move to the vision capture point by PTP motion, and queue with motion commands.

Vision_DoJob_Line.documentation: Execute the existing vision jobs in the project, move to the initial position by line motion, and queue with motion commands.

Vision_IsJobAvailable.documentation: Check if the vision job in the current project is present and valid.

Vision_GetOutputArraySize.documentation: Retrieve the array size of the 2D output variables after vision job execution.
signatures: int|string - Flying job index or point name
int|string - Flying job index or point name

Vision_GetOutputArrayValue.documentation: Retrieve the array values of the 2D output variables after vision job execution.
signatures: int|string - Flying job index or point name
int|string - Flying job index or point name
int|string - Flying job index or point name

Vision_GetTriggerJobOutputCount.documentation: Retrieve the output variable count in the buffer after vision IO Trigger Job execution.

Vision_GetTriggerJobOutputValue.documentation: Retrieve and remove output variable values from the buffer after vision IO Trigger Job execution (FIFO).

ScriptListen.documentation: Enter external script control mode. Returns true on ScriptExit() or project stop; false on timeout or TCP Listener failure.

ScriptExit.documentation: Exit external script control mode and wait for commands to finish before leaving Listen node.

modbus_open.documentation: Open the connection to the Modbus TCP/RTU device.

modbus_close.documentation: Close the connection from the Modbus TCP/RTU device.

modbus_read.documentation: Modbus TCP/RTU read function. Returns data type based on predefined parameters.

modbus_read_int16.documentation: Modbus TCP/RTU read, transform address data to int16 array.

modbus_read_int32.documentation: Modbus TCP/RTU read, transform address data to int32 array.

modbus_read_float.documentation: Modbus TCP/RTU read, transform address data to float array.

modbus_read_double.documentation: Modbus TCP/RTU read, transform address data to double array.

modbus_read_string.documentation: Modbus TCP/RTU read, convert address data to UTF8 string.

modbus_write.documentation: Modbus TCP/RTU write function. Returns true on success, false if empty data or communication error.

svr_read.documentation: Read the item value in the communication data table of Ethernet Slave in the Connection Tab of Robot Setting at the local host. Returns value by set data type.

svr_write.documentation: Write the item value into the communication data table of Ethernet Slave. Returns false if item name does not exist, item is read-only, or value type mismatches.

profinet_read_input.documentation: Read the Profinet input table content. Returns data based on address or item definition in the communication data table.
signatures: int - Amount of addresses to read

profinet_read_input_int.documentation: Read the Profinet input table content and convert data to 32-bit integer. Endian: 0=Little, 1=Big, 2=Config file.

profinet_read_input_float.documentation: Read the Profinet input table content and convert data to 32-bit floating-point. Endian: 0=Little, 1=Big, 2=Config file.

profinet_read_input_string.documentation: Read the Profinet input table content and convert data to UTF8 string (ending with 0x00 encountered).

profinet_read_input_bit.documentation: Read the Profinet input table content and retrieve the nth bit value. Returns 1 for bit==1, 0 for bit==0.
signatures: int - Amount of bits to read
int - Amount of bits to read

profinet_read_output.documentation: Read the Profinet output table content. Returns data based on address or item definition in the communication data table.
signatures: int - Amount of addresses to read

profinet_read_output_int.documentation: Read the Profinet output table content and convert data to 32-bit integer. Endian: 0=Little, 1=Big, 2=Config file.

profinet_read_output_float.documentation: Read the Profinet output table content and convert data to 32-bit floating-point. Endian: 0=Little, 1=Big, 2=Config file.

profinet_read_output_string.documentation: Read the Profinet output table content and convert data to UTF8 string (ending with 0x00 encountered).

profinet_read_output_bit.documentation: Read the Profinet output table content and retrieve the nth bit value. Returns 1 for bit==1, 0 for bit==0.
signatures: int - Amount of bits to read
int - Amount of bits to read

profinet_write_output.documentation: Write data to the Profinet output table. Available data types: byte, byte[], int, int[], float, float[], string. Returns false if empty data or communication error.
signatures: int - Max address amount to write
int - Amount of addresses to write
int - Amount of addresses to write

profinet_write_output_bit.documentation: Write content to the nth bit value of the data byte in the Profinet output table. Bit value=1 for byte>=1, bit value=0 for byte==0.
signatures: int - Starting bit to write data
int - Amount of bits to write
int - Starting bit to write data
int - Starting bit to write data
int - Amount of bits to write
int - Starting bit to write data

eip_read_input.documentation: Read the EtherNet/IP input table content. Returns data based on address or item definition in the communication data table.
signatures: int - Amount of addresses to read

eip_read_input_int.documentation: Read the EtherNet/IP input table content and convert data to 32-bit integer. Endian: 0=Little, 1=Big, 2=Config file.

eip_read_input_float.documentation: Read the EtherNet/IP input table content and convert data to 32-bit floating-point. Endian: 0=Little, 1=Big, 2=Config file.

eip_read_input_string.documentation: Read the EtherNet/IP input table content and convert data to UTF8 string (ending with 0x00 encountered).

eip_read_input_bit.documentation: Read the EtherNet/IP input table content and retrieve the nth bit value. Returns 1 for bit==1, 0 for bit==0.
signatures: int - Amount of bits to read
int - Amount of bits to read

eip_read_output.documentation: Read the EtherNet/IP output table content. Returns data based on address or item definition in the communication data table.
signatures: int - Amount of addresses to read

eip_read_output_int.documentation: Read the EtherNet/IP output table content and convert data to 32-bit integer. Endian: 0=Little, 1=Big, 2=Config file.

eip_read_output_float.documentation: Read the EtherNet/IP output table content and convert data to 32-bit floating-point. Endian: 0=Little, 1=Big, 2=Config file.

eip_read_output_string.documentation: Read the EtherNet/IP output table content and convert data to UTF8 string (ending with 0x00 encountered).

eip_read_output_bit.documentation: Read the EtherNet/IP output table content and retrieve the nth bit value. Returns 1 for bit==1, 0 for bit==0.
signatures: int - Amount of bits to read
int - Amount of bits to read

eip_write_output.documentation: Write data to the EtherNet/IP output table. Available data types: byte, byte[], int, int[], float, float[], string. Returns false if empty data or communication error.
signatures: int - Max address amount to write
int - Amount of addresses to write
int - Amount of addresses to write

eip_write_output_bit.documentation: Write content to the nth bit value of the data byte in the EtherNet/IP output table. Bit value=1 for byte>=1, bit value=0 for byte==0.
signatures: int - Starting bit to write data
int - Amount of bits to write
int - Starting bit to write data
int - Starting bit to write data
int - Amount of bits to write
int - Starting bit to write data

ethercat_read_input.documentation: Read the EtherCAT input table content. Returns data based on address or item definition in the communication data table.
signatures: int - Amount of addresses to read

ethercat_read_input_int.documentation: Read the EtherCAT input table content and convert data to 32-bit integer. Endian: 0=Little, 1=Big, 2=Config file.

ethercat_read_input_float.documentation: Read the EtherCAT input table content and convert data to 32-bit floating-point. Endian: 0=Little, 1=Big, 2=Config file.

ethercat_read_input_string.documentation: Read the EtherCAT input table content and convert data to UTF8 string (ending with 0x00 encountered).

ethercat_read_input_bit.documentation: Read the EtherCAT input table content and retrieve the nth bit value. Returns 1 for bit==1, 0 for bit==0.
signatures: int - Amount of bits to read
int - Amount of bits to read

ethercat_read_output.documentation: Read the EtherCAT output table content. Returns data based on address or item definition in the communication data table.
signatures: int - Amount of addresses to read

ethercat_read_output_int.documentation: Read the EtherCAT output table content and convert data to 32-bit integer. Endian: 0=Little, 1=Big, 2=Config file.

ethercat_read_output_float.documentation: Read the EtherCAT output table content and convert data to 32-bit floating-point. Endian: 0=Little, 1=Big, 2=Config file.

ethercat_read_output_string.documentation: Read the EtherCAT output table content and convert data to UTF8 string (ending with 0x00 encountered).

ethercat_read_output_bit.documentation: Read the EtherCAT output table content and retrieve the nth bit value. Returns 1 for bit==1, 0 for bit==0.
signatures: int - Amount of bits to read
int - Amount of bits to read

ethercat_write_output.documentation: Write data to the EtherCAT output table. Available data types: byte, byte[], int, int[], float, float[], string. Returns false if empty data or communication error.
signatures: int - Max address amount to write
int - Amount of addresses to write
int - Amount of addresses to write

ethercat_write_output_bit.documentation: Write content to the nth bit value of the data byte in the EtherCAT output table. Bit value=1 for byte>=1, bit value=0 for byte==0.
signatures: int - Starting bit to write data
int - Amount of bits to write
int - Starting bit to write data
int - Starting bit to write data
int - Amount of bits to write
int - Starting bit to write data

cclink_read_input.documentation: Read the CC-Link input table content. Return type depends on item definition (byte, byte[], int, int[], float, float[], string).
signatures: int - Amount of addresses to read

cclink_read_input_int16.documentation: Read the CC-Link input table content and convert data to 16-bit integer. Conversion: 0=Little-Endian, 1=Big-Endian, 2=Based on config file.

cclink_read_input_int.documentation: Read the CC-Link input table content and convert data to 32-bit integer. Conversion: 0=Little-Endian, 1=Big-Endian, 2=Based on config file.

cclink_read_input_float.documentation: Read the CC-Link input table content and convert data to 32-bit floating-point. Conversion: 0=Little-Endian, 1=Big-Endian, 2=Based on config file.

cclink_read_input_string.documentation: Read the CC-Link input table content and convert data to UTF8 string (ending with 0x00).

cclink_read_input_bit.documentation: Read the CC-Link input table content and retrieve the nth bit value. Returns 1 for bit=1, 0 for bit=0. Bit data returned as byte array.
signatures: int - Amount of bits to read
int - Amount of bits to read

cclink_read_output.documentation: Read the CC-Link output table content. Return type depends on item definition (byte, byte[], int, int[], float, float[], string).
signatures: int - Amount of addresses to read

cclink_read_output_int16.documentation: Read the CC-Link output table content and convert data to 16-bit integer. Conversion: 0=Little-Endian, 1=Big-Endian, 2=Based on config file.

cclink_read_output_int.documentation: Read the CC-Link output table content and convert data to 32-bit integer. Conversion: 0=Little-Endian, 1=Big-Endian, 2=Based on config file.

cclink_read_output_float.documentation: Read the CC-Link output table content and convert data to 32-bit floating-point. Conversion: 0=Little-Endian, 1=Big-Endian, 2=Based on config file.

cclink_read_output_string.documentation: Read the CC-Link output table content and convert data to UTF8 string (ending with 0x00).

cclink_read_output_bit.documentation: Read the CC-Link output table content and retrieve the nth bit value. Returns 1 for bit=1, 0 for bit=0. Bit data returned as byte array.
signatures: int - Amount of bits to read
int - Amount of bits to read

cclink_write_output.documentation: Write data to the CC-Link output table. Available data types: byte, byte[], int, int[], float, float[], string. Returns true on success, false if data is empty or send/receive fails.
signatures: int - Max address amount to write
int - Amount of addresses to write

cclink_write_output_bit.documentation: Write content to the nth bit value of the data byte in the CC-Link output table. Bit value=1 for byte>=1, bit value=0 for byte==0.
signatures: int - Starting bit to write data
int - Amount of bits to write
int - Starting bit to write data
int - Starting bit to write data
int - Amount of bits to write
int - Starting bit to write data
